title: Mitochondrial ROS Analysis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"gb3od","text":"This protocol is used to measure ROS (H2O2) levels in isolated mitochondria.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"fmf32","text":"- Amplex Red Hydrogen Peroxide/Peroxidase Assay Kit (Invitrogen A22188)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bodmg","text":"- Mitochondrial respiration buffer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"r167x","text":"- Respiratory substrate solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Dilute 30 µg crude mitochondrial preps to 40 µL with mitochondrial respiration buffer.
Prepare positive (10, 5, 2, 1 and 0.5 µM H 2 O 2 ) and negative (1X mitochondrial respiration buffer) controls.

Prepare a working solution of 100 µM Amplex Red reagent and 0.2 U/mL HRP by mixing the following: 50 µL of 10 mM Amplex Red reagent stock solution 100 µL of 10 U/mL HRP stock solution 4.85 mL of 1X reaction buffer
Add 50 µL of the working solution into each well.
Record fluorescence emission at 590 nm (excitation at 560 nm) for 8 min.
Add 10 µL of respiratory substrate into each well; final concentration is 5 mM pyruvate and 2.5 mM malate.
Record fluorescence emission at 590 nm (excitation at 560 nm) for 45 min.

Prepare mitochondrial respiration buffer (100 mL; 120 mM KCl, 5 mM KH 2 PO 4 , 10 mM HEPES, 2 mM MgCl 2 , 1 mM EGTA). 894.6 mg KCl 68.0 mg KH 2 PO 4 238.8 mg HEPES 19.0 mg MgCl 2 38.0 mg EGTA pH 7.2 (adjusted with KOH)
Prepare 10 mM Amplex Red reagent stock solution.
Warm up Amplex Red reagent (blue cap) and DMSO (green cap) at RT.
Immediately before use, add 60 μL of DMSO into a vial of Amplex Red (each vial of Amplex Red is sufficient for 100 assays of 100 μL).
Prepare 1X reaction buffer by diluting 4 mL of 5X reaction buffer (white cap) with 16 mL of deionized water.
Make 10 U/mL horseradish peroxidase (HRP) stock solution.
Reconstitute a vial of HRP (yellow cap) with 1 mL of 1X reaction buffer.
Prepare 20 mM hydrogen peroxide (H 2 O 2 ) working solution.
After the assay, aliquot unused HRP stock solution into single-use tubes and store at -20.
Immediately before use, dilute the ~3% (0.88 M) H 2 O 2 (red cap) into appropriate volume of 1X reactionbuffer to make a 20 mM working solution; exact concentration is on the label.
Prepare respiratory substrate solution (10 mL; 50 mM pyruvate and 25 mM malate). 55.0 mg pyruvate 33.5 mg malate 10 mL of mitochondrial respiration buffer
creator: Szu-Chi Liao
